checks.a_analysis: The output includes a section titled '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' which contains text stating content could not be converted. While this text is not in the source PDF, it is interpreted as a pipeline-generated status message/placeholder for missing content rather than a fabrication of document content. All other text in the output can be traced back to the source document.

The URLs provided in the output (e.g., Slide 12 footnotes) match the source document's content. One URL in Section 2 (Slide 12) includes a '.4' which is a footnote reference appearing as part of the URL due to reformatting, but it is not fabricated.

The first 10 pages of the source document are entirely missing from the output. The JSON explicitly states 'Pages 1-11 unavailable' (though page 11 content is actually present as the first section). Slides 1-10, covering the title, agenda, FGDC background, GDA summary, and initial NC relationship slides, are omitted.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output begins with the content of Slide 11.

checks.a_analysis: Tables in the output generally preserve the row count of corresponding structures in the source. For example, the Goals and Objectives matrix on Slide 30 is faithfully captured as a table with consistent row counts.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Significant content within several slides is omitted. On Slide 15 ('How is the NSDI Being Used?'), the descriptive labels for support functions (e.g., Assessment, Forecasting, Early Warning) and the sector labels (Agriculture, Hazards, Infrastructure, etc.) are missing. On Slide 18, the specific URLs for broadband mapping resources are omitted from the text elements.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide 15 diagram labels: 'Planning', 'Assessment', 'Monitoring', 'Forecasting', 'Early Warning', 'Policy & Compliance', 'Navigation', 'Modeling', 'Mapping', and sector names. Slide 18 URLs: 'broadbandmap.fcc.gov/home' and 'www.nconemap.gov/pages/broadband'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 5 (Slide 15) and Section 8 (Slide 18) lack these textual elements in their content lists.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor differences in whitespace and character normalization (like the smiley face icon on Slide 35) are present but fall within the bounds of acceptable reformatting for conversion.

checks.a_analysis: The output document does not contain duplicated text blocks from the source. Each slide's content appears exactly once in the structured output.

The output maintains a consistent heading hierarchy where slide titles are treated as section titles, and sub-headers within slides (e.g., Vision/Mission on Page 29) are correctly nested as H3 elements. No heading levels are skipped.

The document contains several structural tables, including comment boxes (Pages 13, 16, 23) and a technical matrix (Page 30). These are all correctly identified and converted as tables with proper row/cell logic. For example, the Goals and Objectives matrix on Page 30 correctly identifies the header row and data cells.

The reading order is significantly corrupted. The source PDF begins with Slide 1 on Page 1, but the structured output's first section begins at Slide 10 (Page 11). The first 10 slides of the document are entirely missing from the primary content flow and are instead replaced by a placeholder section at the very end of the JSON declaring them 'unavailable'. This is a major structural failure as the start of the document is moved to the end as a summary disclaimer.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF starts with page 1: 'The National Spatial Data Infrastructure (NSDI)'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The first section in the JSON is 'NSDI: A Digital Infrastructure for the Nation' (Slide 10/Page 11). The missing content is referenced in the final section: '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]'.
checks.b_evidence_location: Initial document sections and Final section (index 28).

checks.a_analysis: Lists throughout the document (bulleted and numbered) are correctly identified and converted. Nested lists, such as those on Page 17 (Examples of Local, State and Federal Data), are properly represented with the 'nested_list' attribute in the JSON.
Hyperlinks present in the source, specifically in the footnotes on Page 12, are correctly captured and converted into clickable link elements with the correct target URLs.

checks.a_analysis: The conversion has failed to include the first 10 pages of the PDF in the body content. The HTML output begins with the content from Slide 10 (PDF page 11). An error message at the end of the HTML confirms that pages 1-11 were not converted and are unavailable. This is a significant omission of the introductory and foundational sections of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 37 pages, starting with the title slide 'The National Spatial Data Infrastructure (NSDI)'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML document starts with the heading 'NSDI: A Digital Infrastructure for the Nation' (Slide 10) and concludes with a section titled '[Pages 1-11 unavailable]' which states 'Content from pages 1-11 could not be converted.'
checks.b_evidence_location: Beginning and end of HTML document

checks.a_analysis: The table titled 'Draft NSDI Goals and Objectives' (Slide 26 / PDF page 30) is rendered correctly as an HTML table. It maintains the relationship between 'Goals' and their corresponding 'Objectives'. For example, Objectives 2.3 and 2.4 are correctly grouped together as they are in the source PDF grid. The table is readable and logically structured.

Nearly all meaningful images, diagrams, and charts from the source PDF are missing in the rendered HTML. Many of these visual elements are replaced by large, solid green rectangular blocks. This includes critical infographics such as 'What does the NSDI consist of?' (Slide 12), 'The NSDI Today' (Slide 19), and various maps and icons on Slide 11 and 15. The loss of these visual aids significantly degrades the information value of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Multiple slides contain complex graphics and diagrams, such as the 'GEODATA LAYERS' diagram on Slide 12 (PDF page 14) and the 'Economic Value of Location' icons on Slide 11 (PDF page 12).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output shows large green blocks instead of images. For instance, after the heading 'What does the NSDI consist of?', there is a large green block. Similarly, Slides 18, 19, 20, 28, 29, and 30 all contain these green placeholder blocks.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, specifically slides 10, 11, 12, 15, 18, 19, 20, 28, 29, 30, 31, 32, and 33

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of body content duplication. While some headings are repeated (e.g., 'Draft NSDI Goals and Objectives'), this accurately reflects the source PDF which uses these headings on multiple slides. Paragraphs and unique content blocks appear only once in their respective sections.
